prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "侧深蹲" (lateral squat / side squat) exercise, front view, facing the viewer. Person posture: Athletic person at the bottom of a lateral squat: feet wider than shoulder-width apart, weight shifted entirely onto the right leg with right knee bent at roughly 90 degrees and right thigh parallel to the floor, right foot flat on the ground; left leg fully extended straight to the side with foot flat on the ground and toe pointing forward; hips pushed back, torso upright with slight forward lean, hands clasped at chest height for balance, plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: quadriceps and gluteus maximus of the bent (right) leg and the adductor muscles of the extended (left) inner thigh. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: gluteus medius of the bent leg and hamstring group. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "股四头肌" pointing to the quadriceps of the bent leg; "臀大肌 / 臀中肌" pointing to the glutes of the bent leg; "内收肌群" pointing to the adductor muscles of the extended inner thigh. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
